page: The UpCloud GPU-16xCPU-192GB-3xL4 is an x86_64 architecture server virtualized via the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 vCPUs with a shared allocation model and is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding 12.0 GB of RAM per core. The server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

This configuration integrates three NVIDIA L4 GPUs based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing a total of 72 GB of VRAM. There is no local storage bundled with this server type, requiring external block storage attachments for operating systems and data persistence.

The shared CPU allocation presents a performance tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, high-intensity CPU processing. However, the high density of GPU accelerators and system memory relative to the CPU count offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy parallel computing tasks that rely primarily on GPU execution rather than dedicated CPU cores.

Appropriate workloads for this server configuration include GPU-accelerated machine learning inference, data science jobs, parallel graphics rendering, and memory-intensive analytical processing where GPU compute is the primary bottleneck.
description: The UpCloud GPU-16xCPU-192GB-3xL4 is an x86_64 virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 16 shared vCPUs and 192.0 GB of system memory, providing 12.0 GB of RAM per core. Acceleration is delivered via three NVIDIA L4 GPUs from the Ada Lovelace architecture, offering a combined 72 GB of VRAM. This server does not include local storage, necessitating external storage solutions. The shared CPU allocation represents a tradeoff for CPU-bound tasks, but the high memory-to-core ratio and triple-GPU setup provide qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that offload processing to the GPU. It is designed for workloads such as machine learning inference, data science tasks, and parallel rendering pipelines.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-16xCPU-192GB-3xL4 is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 16 shared vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and three NVIDIA L4 GPUs with 72 GB total VRAM, designed for GPU-accelerated workloads without local storage.

tagline: An accelerator-focused virtual server combining three NVIDIA L4 GPUs and 192 GB of memory for parallel computing and machine learning workloads.

16 vCPUs utilizing a shared CPU allocation model
192.0 GB of system memory providing 12.0 GB per core
